title: Remove direct model exposure on port 30000
description: Stop exposing llama-server directly to callers. Keep the model reachable only to the API/service path.
acceptance_criteria:
- port 30000 is no longer publicly reachable
- API on 8081 continues to work
- health checks and model supervision still function

title: Set up shareable Git repo without secrets
Improve coworker-facing authentication and request attribution
description: Move beyond a single shared bearer token so requests can be attributed and revoked cleanly.
acceptance_criteria:
- document or implement a stronger auth story than one shared token
- requests can be attributed to individual callers or identities
- invalid callers are rejected cleanly

title: Pre-access hardening before coworker rollout
description:
Track the remaining service hardening work before giving broader access to the malware scan API.

Scope:
- eliminate direct model bypass exposure
- add service-level backpressure
- improve caller accountability
- reduce information exposure in health/debug paths
- decide whether scan-url should remain exposed without persistent caching

title: Add global admission control for uncached scans
description: Protect the shared service from overload when many distinct repos are scanned concurrently.
acceptance_criteria:
- add a global concurrency cap or queue for uncached scans
- overloaded requests fail predictably
- same-key dedupe still works

title: Reduce sensitive internal detail in health/debug responses
description: Trim internal implementation details from the shared health surface.
acceptance_criteria:
- health responses avoid leaking unnecessary internal paths or deployment details
- operational usefulness is preserved

title: Decide whether scan-url remains exposed without persistent caching
description: Evaluate whether scan-url should be kept public as-is, cached, or restricted.
acceptance_criteria:
- scan-url exposure decision is implemented or documented
- repeated use cannot cause unexpected backend waste
